---
title: "Appendix A · Compute and deployment"
---

Implementation detail for the forecast pipeline: which AI weather model, where it executes, and how its
output is staged. None of this changes the physics ([Physics — Hydrology](03a-physics-hydrology.qmd));
it is the machinery that delivers the forcing to it.

## Model selection: which model predicts rain

The budget needs precipitation. Whether an AI weather model actually predicts it is **not** something
that can be assumed from its name, its resolution, or its variable list.

::: {.callout-important}
## `GraphCastOperational` emits `tp06` as a zero-filled placeholder
It is HRES-fine-tuned and does not predict precipitation at all; the `tp06` slot is filled with
`np.zeros` purely to keep the variable list consistent. It is also the *obvious* selection, being the
0.25° "operational" model. Choosing it yields a rainfall forecast that is **identically zero**, and the
water budget will forecast a drought with **no error raised**.

`assert_precipitation_is_real()` refuses it by name, refuses any all-zero field, and is pinned by a
test. A forecast that is exactly zero everywhere is never a physical outcome over a PNW wet-season
window — it is a bug.
:::

Across earth2studio's prognostic models, **FuXi is the only other model that natively emits `tp06`**.
Pangu, FengWu, Aurora, SFNO, FCN3 and ACE2 emit no precipitation at all, and require the
`PrecipitationAFNO` diagnostic to be chained on.

| | GraphCastSmall | **FuXi** (selected) |
|---|---|---|
| precipitation | `tp06` | `tp06` |
| grid | 1° (~110 × 75 km) | **0.25°** (~28 × 19 km) |
| horizon | ~10 d | **cascade trained for 5 / 10 / 15 d** |
| runtime dependency | `jax[cuda13]` → GPU-only | **onnxruntime** |

FuXi covers the +1…+15 day horizon by design, at 4× the resolution, and is not CUDA-JAX-locked.

### Adapter guards

Two failure modes are guarded rather than assumed away:

- **Zero / placeholder precipitation** — refused by name and by magnitude (above).
- **Unit convention.** The `tp06` field is accumulated **metres** in some checkpoints and millimetres in
  others. A 6-hour total of 0.02 m is a heavy but ordinary PNW event; the same number read as
  millimetres is a drizzle, and read the other way, 20 mm becomes 20 m of rain. The adapter decides on
  magnitude and **logs which branch it took**, rather than guessing silently.

## Execution: GPU

GraphCast cannot run on macOS/arm64 at all — its earth2studio extra requires `jax[cuda13]`. FuXi is
onnxruntime-based and imports on CPU/arm64, but its weights are **9.43 GB** (a three-model cascade) and
CPU inference over 60 six-hourly steps is impractical. It is a cluster job.

Forecasts are generated on **UW Hyak Tillicum (H200)** via SkyPilot (`deploy/tillicum-fuxi.yaml`),
following `gaia-hazlab/skypilot-hyak` conventions:

```bash
pixi run stage-mvp                 # reports which initialisations are missing from Kopah
sky launch --infra slurm/tillicum -c fuxi deploy/tillicum-fuxi.yaml \
  --env INITS="2025-09-01,2025-09-08,..." \
  --env AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID --env AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
```

A **rolling weekly initialisation** across the wet season is run as a **single job**: the 9.43 GB weight
cascade is pulled once and cached, so 29 initialisations are not 29 downloads. One failing
initialisation warns and the sweep continues rather than losing the run.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Slurm nodes do not autostop
`time` is a hard kill: the node is terminated at expiry, finished or not. It must be sized for the
**whole sweep**, not one initialisation. (The `debug` QoS caps runtime at 1 h, which is far too short.)
:::

The GPU job writes only the forcing store. The soil-state forecast is pure NumPy and runs anywhere
afterwards; nothing is copied by hand.

## Staging: Zarr on Kopah

Staging follows `gaia-hazlab/gaia-cli` so that the stores interoperate: obstore +
`zarr.storage.ObjectStore`, `zarr_format=3`, `consolidated=False`, ~10 MB chunks, credentials from
`AWS_PROFILE`, and the Kopah endpoint via `AWS_ENDPOINT_URL`.

```
s3://gaia/soil-twin/static/svm_cvm17.zarr        SVM shallow-Vs grid
s3://gaia/soil-twin/static/vs30_90m.zarr         derived Vs30
s3://gaia/soil-twin/forecast/fuxi/<init>.zarr    AI rainfall forcing
```

**Why object-stored Zarr rather than files.** The USGS CVM17 SVM grid is distributed as a **6.2 GB
netCDF that must be downloaded in full** — and is captcha-gated, so the download cannot even be
scripted — before a single Vs30 value can be read. Staged as chunked Zarr it is **756 MB** and is read
lazily: only the analysis window, only the shallow levels. Vs30 re-derived from the Zarr is
bit-identical to the netCDF-derived field.

Chunks are 256 × 256 spatial tiles × **full depth**, because every read is "a window, all depths" — the
shape of the travel-time integral that defines Vs30.

::: {.callout-note}
## Two staging constraints, recorded
- SkyPilot `file_mounts` **do not work** with Kopah's custom endpoint. The S3 API is used directly
  rather than mounting.
- A chunking helper that overrides a caller's dask chunks makes one Zarr chunk span several dask
  chunks. **xarray refuses this outright**, because writing it in parallel can corrupt data.
  Dask-backed variables must keep the caller's chunking.
:::

## Observed forcing and the analysis window

`src/data/fetch_prism_daily.py` reads the `gaia-hazlab/prism-stac` catalog via `odc.stac`, falling back
to the PRISM service directly for windows the catalog does not cover. It pulls precipitation and
`tmean` — the latter required by the snow partition — and adds Hamon PET per day.

`pixi run stage-mvp` stages the four streams of an analysis window together, because only together do
they close the budget:

| stream | source | role |
|---|---|---|
| **forcing** | PRISM daily | what went in |
| **state** | NWIS wells | where the water table sat |
| **flux** | USGS gauges (+ baseflow separation) | what came out |
| **forecast** | FuXi on Kopah | what we predict would go in |

The forecast is the only stream that is not an observation; the other three exist to score it. When
initialisations are missing from Kopah, `stage-mvp` reports which and emits the exact launch command —
it does not fabricate a forcing.
